sections.heading: The Core Mission
sections.body: Archive hateful/far-right posts and comments from local communities so there is an immutable record. After the Civil War, there was no social media to scrape. Now there is. We can know who the people in our world are. No plausible deniability later — 'they can't say they weren't.'

sections.heading: Why This Matters Now
sections.body: Social media captures what ordinary people say and do in real-time. Every racist Nextdoor post, every homophobic Facebook comment, every 'go back where you came from' on a local subreddit — it's all evidence of who people were when they thought no one was keeping track. After the next civil conflict, there is no erasing this record.

sections.heading: Two-pronged Collection Approach
sections.body:
**Platforms with APIs (Reddit, X, Telegram)** — traditional scraping via their APIs where available.

**Locked-down platforms (Facebook, Nextdoor)** — browser-based vision agent approach using Owl Alpha (free on OpenRouter). The agent logs in, browses like a human, screenshots posts, feeds them to a vision model for classification.

sections.heading: Audience Lenses Explored
sections.body:
**Progressive candidates & campaigns** — alert system + rapid response templates. "3 new anti-trans posts in your district today — here's how to respond."

**Journalists** — searchable evidence database + story leads. "These accounts are brigading city council comments across swing states."

**Activists & orgs** — tracking dashboards over time. "The school board takeover narrative peaked 3 weeks before the election."

**General public** — transparency-as-accountability map.

sections.heading: Product Shapes Considered
sections.body:
**City Watch** — pick one city, scrape every platform, produce weekly reports. First report IS the demo.

**Narrative Tracker** — trace a hate narrative from fringe to mainstream (4chan → X → city council candidate's speech). This is genuinely novel.

**Candidate Shield** — watch what's said about progressive candidates in their district. Real-time alerts + counter-narrative auto-generation.

**Public Map** — geographic heatmap of hate incidents by city. Click a dot → see the evidence. Immutable, timestamped, linked. Most viral potential.

sections.heading: Tech Architecture: Two-Tier Collection
sections.body:
**Tier 1 — API scraping:**
- Reddit via PRAW (public API, easy)
- X via API v2 (rate-limited on free tier)
- Telegram via Telethon (public channel search)

**Tier 2 — Browser vision agent:**
- Facebook + Nextdoor: Hermes browser logs in as human
- Screenshots each post → feeds to Owl Alpha on OpenRouter
- Prompt: "Does this post contain hate speech, dog whistles, disinformation, or calls to organize against marginalized groups?"
- Results saved to DB with screenshot, URL, timestamp

**Constraints:**
- Slow (~15-30s per post, 50 posts = 15-25 min per platform)
- Facebook sessions invalidate often → need burner account
- Owl Alpha is free now but won't be forever
- One city at a time at this scale

sections.heading: Pilot City: Clearwater, FL
sections.body:
**Why Clearwater:** Pinellas County swing district, active local politics, home to Scientology HQ (unique angle). Target for **City Watch** approach — one metro area, all platforms.

**Target subreddits:**
- r/ClearwaterFl — local sub, low volume, watch for school board / city council threads
- r/tampa — regional, higher volume, spills over often
- r/florida — state-level, active hate speech around FL politics
- r/StPetersburgFL — adjacent city, progressive vs conservative dynamics

**Reddit status:** Public JSON API rate-limits hard from WSL. Need PRAW with Reddit API app credentials to get 100 req/min.

**Collected resident comments:** 19 comments from 4 high-engagement threads (Scientology, city council, local politics). Saved to ~/scraping-hade/clearwater-reddit-posts.json.

**Incident evidence:** 9 verified hate incidents from local news. Saved to ~/scraping-hate/clearwater-findings.json.

**Next:** Install PRAW for proper Reddit API access, X/Twitter search via xurl, set up cron job for weekly snapshots.
